# CLAUDE.md

Meesho fork of `ongakuer/CircleIndicator` plus the separately-vendored `imbryk/LoopingViewPager`. The repo publishes one library AAR (`com.meesho.android:circle-indicator`) to JFrog Artifactory; the AAR is consumed by the Meesho Android monorepo and other internal projects. Active branch is `master`; `main` is frozen at 2023-05.

## Architecture

Three Gradle modules in `settings.gradle`: `:circleindicator`, `:LoopingViewPager`, `:sample`. Only one ships.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ :circleindicator         ─── PUBLISHED               │  → JFrog Artifactory
│   • Java only, no DI, no convention plugin           │     com.meesho.android:circle-indicator:<tag-or-SNAPSHOT>
│   • Applies com.android.library + jfrog              │
│     artifactory + maven-publish                      │
│   • Self-contained (no project() deps)               │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ :LoopingViewPager        ─── NOT PUBLISHED           │
│   • Vendored from imbryk/LoopingViewPager            │
│     (Apache 2.0, Copyright 2013 Leszek Mzyk)         │
│   • Java only; package com.imbryk.viewPager          │
│   • Consumed only by :sample                         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ :sample                  ─── DEMO / REGRESSION APP   │
│   • depends on :circleindicator + :LoopingViewPager  │
│   • One fragment per feature (Default, ChangeColor,  │
│     CustomAnimation, DynamicAdapter, ResetAdapter,   │
│     LoopViewPager, SnackbarBehavior) — adding a      │
│     library feature without a matching fragment      │
│     leaves it without a manual regression case.      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The root `build.gradle` applies `com.jfrog.artifactory` and `maven-publish` to **every** subproject via `allprojects { … }`. Only `:circleindicator` declares a `publishing { publications { aar … } }` block, so it's the only artifact that actually publishes. Adding a similar block elsewhere starts publishing that module — be deliberate.

## Public surface (what consumers consume)

Maven coordinate: **`com.meesho.android:circle-indicator:<tag-or-SNAPSHOT>`**.

**Classes (`me.relex.circleindicator.*` — package preserved from upstream; part of public ABI):**

- **`CircleIndicator`** (extends `android.widget.LinearLayout`) — the headline widget.
  - `setViewPager(ViewPager)` — primary entry point. Attaches an internal `OnPageChangeListener`; safe to call repeatedly (removes previous listener first).
  - `getDataSetObserver()` — returns an internal `DataSetObserver` consumers must register manually on the adapter when the adapter is dynamic: `mAdapter.registerDataSetObserver(indicator.getDataSetObserver())`. **Since upstream v1.2.0 the indicator does NOT auto-register** — adding auto-register back regresses every dynamic-adapter consumer (the v1.2.0 release notes call this out explicitly).
  - `setOnPageChangeListener(OnPageChangeListener)` — `@Deprecated`; use `ViewPager.addOnPageChangeListener` directly.
  - `createIndicators(int count, int currentPosition)` — manual refresh entry point.
  - `animatePageSelected(int position)` — animate a specific position into the "selected" state.
  - `dip2px(float)` — px utility, retained for binary compatibility.
- **`SnackbarBehavior`** (`CoordinatorLayout.Behavior<CircleIndicator>`) — translates the indicator up when a `Snackbar` overlaps it. Wired in XML via `app:layout_behavior="me.relex.circleindicator.SnackbarBehavior"` on a `CircleIndicator` child of `CoordinatorLayout`. Requires the Material library on the consumer's runtime classpath (referenced via `Snackbar.SnackbarLayout`).

**XML styleable attributes** (declared in `circleindicator/src/main/res/values/attrs.xml`):

| Attr | Format | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `ci_width` / `ci_height` / `ci_margin` | `dimension` | `5dp` (derived from `DEFAULT_INDICATOR_WIDTH`) |
| `ci_drawable` / `ci_drawable_unselected` | `reference` | `@drawable/white_radius` (white oval) |
| `ci_animator` / `ci_animator_reverse` | `reference` | `@animator/scale_with_alpha` (alpha 0.5→1, scale 1.0→1.8, `config_shortAnimTime`) |
| `ci_orientation` | enum: `horizontal` / `vertical` | `horizontal` |
| `ci_gravity` | full Android gravity flag set | `center` |

**Default resources that consumers may shadow** by declaring the same resource ID in their own module: `@drawable/white_radius`, `@animator/scale_with_alpha`. Shadowing is the supported customisation path when XML attributes are insufficient.

**Transitive compile classpath leaked to consumers** (declared `implementation` on the library, but still visible at compile time because of how AAR consumers resolve): `androidx.annotation:annotation:1.3.0`, `androidx.legacy:legacy-support-core-ui:1.0.0`, `com.google.android.material:material:1.2.1`. Material is **required at runtime** because of `SnackbarBehavior`; consumers that omit it crash at first inflate.

`:LoopingViewPager` is **not part of the public surface** — it lives in this repo only so `:sample` can demo `CircleIndicator` against a looping pager. To use it in production, vendor it separately or publish it deliberately.

## NEVER DO

- **NEVER** push to `main` — `main` is frozen at 2023-05-09; all active development is on `master`. CI / JFrog publish targets `master`, so anything landed on `main` ships nothing.
- **NEVER** change `:circleindicator`'s publish coordinate away from `com.meesho.android:circle-indicator` — downstream consumers in the Meesho Android monorepo resolve that exact GAV; reverting to upstream `me.relex` breaks every consumer at dependency-resolution time.
- **NEVER** rename the Java packages `me.relex.circleindicator` or `com.imbryk.viewPager` — consumer XML layouts reference the classes by FQN (e.g. `<me.relex.circleindicator.CircleIndicator …/>`); a rename silently breaks every consumer at inflate time. The package names are part of the public ABI even though the Maven groupId is `com.meesho.android`.
- **NEVER** re-introduce auto-`registerDataSetObserver` inside `CircleIndicator.setViewPager` — upstream v1.2.0 deliberately removed it because it caused observer leaks on adapter swap. Every dynamic-adapter consumer now calls `mAdapter.registerDataSetObserver(indicator.getDataSetObserver())` themselves; re-adding the auto-register double-registers the observer in those call sites.
- **NEVER** `git commit --no-verify` (or `--no-gpg-sign`) — bypasses the TruffleHog secret scan declared in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`; a leaked credential is committed silently.
- **NEVER** drop the `jcenter()` repository declaration in the root `build.gradle` without first proving every dependency still resolves — `legacy-support-core-ui:1.0.0` and other AGP-3.4.2-era artifacts may resolve via jcenter only. jcenter has been read-only / sunset since 2021; removing it has worked historically *because* the local Gradle cache is warm.
- **NEVER** run `:circleindicator:artifactoryPublish` against the release repo from a working tree whose `versionName` resolves to `<branch>-SNAPSHOT` — `:circleindicator/build.gradle` picks `RELEASE_REPO_NAME` only when `git tag --points-at HEAD` matches `[0-9.]*[0-9]`. Tag first, then publish.
- **NEVER** bump Android Gradle Plugin past 3.4.2 in this repo without an explicit upgrade plan — the publish block, jcenter dependency, and Java-toolchain-absent build script were all written against AGP 3.x. AGP ≥ 7 will reject the current `buildscript` / repository layout outright.

## Release procedure

`:circleindicator` releases by **git tag**, not by editing `build.gradle`:

1. Land changes on `master`.
2. `git tag <X.Y.Z>` on the commit to release (semver; the regex is `[0-9.]*[0-9]`).
3. `git push origin <X.Y.Z>`.
4. With `JFROG_ARTIFACTORY_URL`, `JFROG_ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME`, `JFROG_ARTIFACTORY_KEY`, `RELEASE_REPO_NAME`, `SNAPSHOT_REPO_NAME` in `~/.gradle/gradle.properties` (or passed via `-P`), run `./gradlew :circleindicator:artifactoryPublish`.

The `versionName()` closure in `:circleindicator/build.gradle` resolves to the tag → publish routes to `RELEASE_REPO_NAME`. Without the tag, the closure resolves to `<branch>-SNAPSHOT` and publish routes to `SNAPSHOT_REPO_NAME`. The hard-coded `version = "2.1.3"` line above the closure is a fallback default; the closure overrides it at publish time.

## Stack

| Concern | Choice | Where enforced |
|---|---|---|
| Build | Android Gradle Plugin **3.4.2** | root `build.gradle` `buildscript.dependencies` |
| Repositories | `google()` + `jcenter()` (sunset; cache-only) | root `build.gradle` `allprojects.repositories` |
| Publish | `com.jfrog.artifactory` + `maven-publish` | root `allprojects` block; `:circleindicator` is the only publisher |
| Versioning | Tag-driven via the `versionName()` closure | `:circleindicator/build.gradle` |
| Secret scan | TruffleHog via `pre-commit` and `pre-push` | `.pre-commit-config.yaml` → `pre-commit-scripts/trufflehog-hook.sh` |
| compileSdk / minSdk / targetSdk | 28 / 14 / 28 | each module's `build.gradle` |
| Language | Java only (no Kotlin) | each module's `build.gradle` |

## Invariants

- **Two upstream forks coexist.** `:circleindicator` is forked from `github.com/ongakuer/CircleIndicator` (last upstream sync 1.2.2 per `CHANGELOG.md`; local at 2.1.3 + Meesho commits). `:LoopingViewPager` is forked from `imbryk/LoopingViewPager` (Apache 2.0, 2013). Both packages are preserved at their upstream paths — they're part of public ABI.
- **JFrog credentials read from project properties, not env vars.** `:circleindicator/build.gradle` publish block expects them on `project.properties`. Missing keys surface as a **null-URL error**, not a credential error — that distinction matters when triaging "publish failed" reports.
- **Pre-commit hooks are auto-installed via a `post-commit` hook.** `.pre-commit-config.yaml` declares stages `pre-commit` and `pre-push` for TruffleHog; `pre-commit-scripts/runner.sh` and `post-commit-scripts/runner.sh` fan out their respective `*.sh` neighbours in parallel. Adding a new hook script means dropping it into one of those directories — no other registration needed.
- **No DI, no Hilt, no convention plugin.** Every module's `build.gradle` applies `com.android.library` (or `com.android.application` for `:sample`) directly. The classes are plain Android `View` / `CoordinatorLayout.Behavior` instances created by XML inflation. Adding DI without a concrete consumer need breaks the inflate-by-XML path.
